0.q: SacB, encoding levansucrase involved in fructan biosynthesis in Bacillus subtilis... The D5-20 and D5-21 lines also had higher concentrations of fructan than the control under both normal and stress conditions.

1.q: ProA enzyme activity can be bypassed as Δ1-pyrroline-5-carboxylate can be converted into L-proline by Δ1-pyrroline-5-carboxylate reductase... only a proA rocD double-mutant of B. subtilis exhibits a tight L-proline auxotrophic growth phenotype.

2.q: It was demonstrated to belong to the SigB operon... Most recently, it was shown to be under exclusive SigB regulation.

3.q: The B. subtilis clpC gene was induced in response to various stresses, including heat shock. A clpC insertional mutant had an impaired tolerance for heat shock and salt stress.

4.q: This allows the rocD-encoded ornithine aminotransferase to convert increased amounts of L-ornithine, an intermediate in L-arginine biosynthesis, to produce increased amounts of γ-glutamate-semialdehyde/Δ1-pyrroline-5-carboxylate.

5.q: The osmostress adaptive L-proline biosynthetic route (ProJ-ProA-ProH) consists of isoenzymes of the first (ProJ) and last (ProH) step of the anabolic L-proline biosynthetic route but shares with it the γ-glutamyl phosphate reductase (ProA).

11.q: yuaA (ktrA) and ykqB (ktrC) encode homologues to the V. alginolyticus KtrA protein.

12.q: yubG (ktrB) and ykrM (ktrD) encode homologues to the V. alginolyticus KtrB protein.

13.q: Firmicutes including Bacillus subtilis encode two 6S RNA paralogs, 6S-1 and 6S-2 RNA.

14.q: A strain defective in both KtrAB and KtrCD exhibited only a residual K+ uptake activity, demonstrating that KtrAB and KtrCD systems are the major K+ transporters of B. subtilis.

21.q: Quantitative RT-PCR data revealed transcriptional upregulation of biofilm marker genes such as tasA, epsA and bslA in the ∆6S-2 RNA strain, particularly during transition from exponential to stationary growth phase.

22.q: Expression of yrhJ, which encodes a cytochrome P450/NADPH reductase, was induced in response to acid and vancomycin.

23.q: As ytvA deletion resulted in reduction of sB activation by several stresses, YtvA is also recognized as a positive regulator of sB activity
